page_lines: Attention: The Franklin County Registry of Deeds is now offering a free fraud alert tool to help property owners receive alerts whenever a document, such as a deed or mortgage, is recorded in their name. Sign up now at
Beginning January 1, 2026, the Maine Registers of Deeds Association will be using a new flat rate recording fee structure.
Real Estate Transfer Tax Declaration Form

The purpose of the Registry of Deeds is to record and preserve all land records in Franklin County. The Franklin County Registry of Deeds has documents dating back to 1838 and the public has access to them all through a searchable index that contains the names of all parties related to the document.
To access and make copies of our records, the public may either visit the Registry of Deeds office in person and use our computer station, books, or registry staff
view and download the documents online using a personal computer.
The public has full access to the same records available to anyone using the computer search screens at the Registry of Deeds office.
The following are available on our research website:
Scanned Documents and Indexes from 1983 forward.
Indexes from 1838 to 1982 have been scanned into the database in their original form. You may search by name and check the original index pages.
Use the links below to access Franklin County’s research website or the websites from any other Registry of Deeds office in the State of Maine.
**Please note: our research website uses Popups and certain functions will not work if you have Popups disabled.
